accordion_sections.heading: Diabetes Referral Guidelines
accordion_sections.html:
Who to refer

Emergencies

Any possible new diagnosis of Type 1 Diabetes:

  • Age <18 years and/or
  • Clinically unwell and/or
  • Weight loss and/or
  • Rapid onset symptoms over days or weeks and/or
  • Ketonuria / ketonaemia.

Any person with existing Type 1 Diabetes who is acutely unwell and at risk of DKA should be signposted to call the Diabetes Triage Helpline on 01387 244 214, Monday to Friday 9am-3.30pm, for Sick Day Rules advice.

Adults: contact the on-call Diabetes & Endocrinology phone on ext 32186, 09:00-16:30 Monday to Friday. Outwith these times, contact Acute Medicine in the usual way.

Children under 16: contact the Paediatrics Team in the usual way.

Urgent

  • Any pregnant woman with a new diagnosis of Type 1 or Type 2 Diabetes.
  • New diagnosis of gestational diabetes in a pregnant woman, usually referred by community midwives through the antenatal pathway.

Make an urgent SCI Gateway referral, commence 5 mg folic acid, and stop medications contraindicated in pregnancy.

Routine

  • Individuals with established Type 1 Diabetes all have access to Annual Screening and Patient Initiated Follow Up to arrange their own appointments by calling 0345 602 2812. The service will endeavour to see them within 4 weeks of their call.
  • Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes aged <35 years.
  • Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes not meeting age / frailty appropriate HbA1c target despite maximal tolerated oral agents (Metformin, SGLT-2 and GLP-1) and who may require injectable therapy.
  • Individuals with diabetes complications requiring specialist input, e.g. peripheral neuropathy or CKD.
  • Individuals where the type of diabetes is uncertain.
  • Individuals with suspected secondary diabetes, e.g. chronic pancreatitis.
  • Individuals with suspected / confirmed monogenic diabetes.
  • Women with Type 1 Diabetes planning pregnancy.
  • Individuals with steroid induced hyperglycaemia who have suboptimal management on maximum tolerated oral agents, as specified in local steroid therapy management guidelines.

Who not to refer

  • Optimisation of non-insulin drug therapies to improve HbA1c: use local Type 2 Diabetes (Adults) guidelines.
  • Individuals with diabetes or pre-diabetes requiring help with weight loss only: see Weight Management in Diabetes and Let's Prevent.
  • Individuals with diabetes under age 16: contact the Paediatrics Team in the usual way.
  • Diabetes retinal screening: this is automatically generated as long as the diagnosis is recorded appropriately on SCI-Diabetes.

How to refer

  • Emergency referrals: contact the on-call Diabetes & Endocrinology phone on ext 32186, 09:00-16:30 Monday to Friday. Outwith these times, contact Acute Medicine in the usual way. Then complete an urgent SCI Gateway referral.
  • General referrals: SCI Gateway.
  • Advice only: SCI Gateway. The service does not operate an email advice service.

Information to include with referral

Referrals without sufficient information will be returned.

  • HbA1c within 3 months.
  • Urinalysis for ketonuria / finger-prick capillary blood ketone.
  • BMI within 3 months.
  • Acute symptoms of diabetes such as rapid weight loss.
  • Known complications of diabetes.
  • Blood pressure.
  • Urine ACR.
  • Smoking status.
  • Lipid profile.

accordion_sections.heading: Patient Initiated Follow Up and Annual Screening
accordion_sections.html:
Individuals with established Type 1 Diabetes all have access to the Annual Screening and Patient Initiated Follow Up system to arrange their own appointments.

  • Contact 0345 602 2812.
  • The service will endeavour to see them within 4 weeks of their call.

Editor note: the email refers to a proposed Word document for this section, but only the Diabetes Referral Guidelines and Weight Management in Diabetes documents were available in the supplied files for this draft.

accordion_sections.heading: Type 2 Diabetes (Adults)
accordion_sections.html:
This section should sit third on the main diabetes page.

Digital Diabetes Remission Programme / Counterweight Plus: make this offer visible for eligible people with Type 2 Diabetes where weight-management or remission support is the primary need.

Referral interface from the Diabetes Referral Guidelines

  • Use local Type 2 Diabetes (Adults) guidance for optimisation of non-insulin drug therapies to improve HbA1c.
  • Consider specialist referral for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes aged <35 years.
  • Consider specialist referral where Type 2 Diabetes is not meeting age / frailty appropriate HbA1c target despite maximal tolerated oral agents (Metformin, SGLT-2 and GLP-1) and injectable therapy may be required.

Editor note: detailed Type 2 Diabetes (Adults) content was not included in the supplied documents. This section captures the requested position and the referral interfaces from the Diabetes Referral Guidelines document.

accordion_sections.heading: Diabetic Foot
accordion_sections.html:
Placeholder for the Diabetic Foot page requested in the email.

Editor note: content is awaiting update from Dr Louise Overend.

accordion_sections.heading: Weight Management in Diabetes
accordion_sections.html:
Individuals with diabetes can be referred, or can self-refer, for Tier 2 or Tier 3 weight management and the Counterweight Plus remission programme for Type 2 Diabetes.

  • For further information see Diabetes Remission - RefHelp.

Individuals with pre-diabetes can be referred for Type 2 Diabetes prevention and pre-diabetes education.

  • See Pre-Diabetes - RefHelp.

accordion_sections.heading: Steroid Induced Hyperglycaemia
accordion_sections.html:
This page can sit within the diabetes section.

  • Individuals with steroid induced hyperglycaemia who have suboptimal management on maximum tolerated oral agents should be considered for referral, as specified in local steroid therapy management guidelines.

Editor note: source content is to be taken from the Clinical Handbook; that source was not included in the supplied files for this draft.

accordion_sections.heading: Let's Prevent
accordion_sections.html:
Individuals with pre-diabetes can be referred for the Type 2 Diabetes prevention and pre-diabetes education programme.

  • See Pre-Diabetes - RefHelp.

Editor note: the email does not specify a fixed position for Let's Prevent within the diabetes page; it has been placed after Steroid Induced Hyperglycaemia for this draft.
